summary: WriteResult getters should throw exception if unacknowledged
description: This is already done in PHPLIB (per the CRUD spec). Doing so for PHPC would allow us to remove the current nullable integer return type.

summary: Error when "modifiers" option is passed by reference
Remove deprecated Query constructor options
description:
The following options for [MongoDB\Driver\Query::__construct()|https://www.php.net/manual/en/mongodb-driver-query.construct.php] are deprecated and can be removed:

 * {{partial}} is a deprecated alias for {{allowPartialResults}}
 * {{maxScan}} was deprecated in server 4.0 (SERVER-32953) and removed in 4.2 (SERVER-35044)
 * {{modifiers}} is used for legacy [query modifiers|https://www.mongodb.com/docs/v5.0/reference/operator/query-modifier/], which are all deprecated.
 * {{oplogReplay}} has been deprecated since PHPC 1.8 (PHPC-1525). It is deprecated in the CRUD spec and ignored by server 4.4+ (SERVER-36186)
 * {{snapshot}} was deprecated in server 3.6 (SERVER-32173) and removed in 4.0 (SERVER-32174)
 * Negative {{limit}} implying {{singleBatch: true}}

summary: Throw if generic boolean URI option has an unexpected type
description:
Historically, the driver does not throw when encountering a non-boolean value for a boolean option. Instead, the driver [uses {{bson_iter_as_bool()}} to cast the value|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/1.2.9/php_phongo.c#L986].

This is inconsistent with our handling of other options (including specific boolean options such as "journal"), which use {{BSON_ITER_HOLDS_TYPE()}} macros to guard processing. PHPC-887 introduces exceptions for unexpected types, but earlier versions of the driver silently ignore options with unexpected types.

Throwing exceptions for non-boolean types that are currently cast and applied would be a BC break, so this ticket has been triaged for the next major version.

summary: Remove deprecated Manager constructor options
description:
Remove the following deprecated URI options:

 * {{canonicalizeHostname}} is a deprecated alias for the "CANONICALIZE_HOST_NAME" property of the {{authMechanismProperties}} URI option
 * {{gssapiServiceName}} is a deprecated alias for the "SERVICE_NAME" property of the {{authMechanismProperties}} URI option
 * {{safe}} is a deprecated alias for a subset of {{w}} option values
 * {{ssl}} is a deprecated alias for the {{tls}} option

Note: these options would likely still be parsed in the connection string by libmongoc, and may also be parsed through our URI options array since we delegate to libmongoc for options with scalar types; however, we could consider explicit logic to _not_ parse them in the URI options array. At the very least, we can stop documenting these options.

----

Remove the following deprecated driver options:

 * {{allow_invalid_hostname}} is a deprecated alias for the {{tlsAllowInvalidHostnames}} URI option
 * {{ca_file}} is a deprecated alias for the {{tlsCAFile}} URI option
 * {{context}} is used for [SSL context options|https://www.php.net/manual/en/context.ssl.php], which are all deprecated
 * {{pem_file}} is a deprecated alias for the {{tlsCertificateKeyFile}} URI option
 * {{pem_pwd}} is a deprecated alias for the {{tlsCertificateKeyFilePassword}} URI option
 * {{weak_cert_validation}} is a deprecated alias for the {{tlsAllowInvalidCertificates}} URI option

summary: WriteException stub should inherit ServerException
description:
According to the [WriteException docs|https://www.php.net/manual/en/class.mongodb-driver-exception-writeexception.php], the class extends ServerException; however, the corresponding stub in the 1.x branch suggests that it extends RuntimeException. The actual implementation in [WriteException.c|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/v1.x/src/MongoDB/Exception/WriteException.c] correctly uses ServerException.

This issue persisted in [BulkWriteException.stub.php|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/v2.x/src/MongoDB/Exception/BulkWriteException.stub.php] after removing WriteException in PHPC-2348.

summary: BulkWriteException stub should inherit ServerException
description:
According to the [WriteException docs|https://www.php.net/manual/en/class.mongodb-driver-exception-writeexception.php], the class extends ServerException; however, the corresponding stub in the 1.x branch suggests that it extends RuntimeException. The actual implementation in [WriteException.c|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/v1.x/src/MongoDB/Exception/WriteException.c] correctly uses ServerException.

This issue persisted in [BulkWriteException.stub.php|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/v2.x/src/MongoDB/Exception/BulkWriteException.stub.php] after removing WriteException in PHPC-2348.

summary: Investigate changes in CDRIVER-5869: Client bulk write can crash when reporting insert ids on verbose results
Accept integer types for Document array access
description:
When modeling BulkWriteCommandResult, the operation results are BSON documents with numeric keys corresponding to the operation from the executed BulkWriteCommand. In my initial implementation of BulkWriteCommandResult, I decided to return the BSON documents directly as {{MongoDB\BSON\Document}} instances. PHPLIB can later utilize a codec to make working with these documents more convenient.

The Document ArrayAccess methods and dimension handlers have historically required a string parameter type. Allowing an integer, which is then internally cast to a string, would make accessing results more convenient when working only with PHPC. No changes will be made for the strictly-typed API (e.g. {{get()}}) or property handlers (which receive {{zend_string}} parameters).

PackedArray has similar logic that enforces an integer argument, but that can remain as-is.

summary: Detect BSON_HAVE_ALIGNED_ALLOC in build process
description:
Add detection of {{BSON_HAVE_ALIGNED_ALLOC}} in the Autotools build process. This constant was introduced by CDRIVER-5876.

Based on the original logic in [mongodb/mongo-c-driver@2a35499|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/commit/2a35499c2ac9e66da7cc6eb3223b459f63fba458], it looks like this can remain disabled for Windows.

summary: BulkWrite::insert() should not append document if PHONGO_BSON_RETURN_ID fails
description:
In [mongodb/mongo-php-driver#1763|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/1763#discussion_r1919737051] (PHPC-2478), [~andreas.braun@mongodb.com] pointed out that an unlikely failure of {{php_phongo_zval_to_bson()}} to populate {{bson_out}} for {{PHONGO_BSON_RETURN_ID}} would result in throwing an exception _after_ the document was already appended to the bulk write. 

That code for {{BulkWriteCommand::insertOne()}} was modeled after existing code in {{BulkWrite::insert()}}, so this ticket can serve to address the original logic in {{BulkWrite::insert()}}.

summary: Investigate changes in CDRIVER-5915: bson_t larger than half maximum size can't be allocated
description:
All bson_t append operations fail if the resulting size is larger than 0x40000000. The allocation size is always rounded up to a power of two, and the next power of two 0x80000000 would be larger than the max bson size, so allocation fails. This makes it impossible to test the true edge cases for allocation size.

An additional bug in test_bson_reserve_buffer_errors is hidden by the allocation bug. That test requires allocation failure in a case that in fact matches the maximum size exactly. It only fails currently due to the internal round-up to 0x80000000.

summary: Disallow comma character in authMechanismProperties connection string value
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2915}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Use git submodule for drivers-evg-tools
description:
drivers-evergreen-tools has seen changes that break builds at times. It would be better if we could manually upgrade drivers-evg-tools instead of auto-updating (through checking out the latest version during the build) to avoid random issues when it's updated upstream.

The GitHub Actions workflows that use drivers-evg-tools also need to import actions from the submodule, not from the repository.

summary: Narrow Serializable::bsonSerialize() tentative return type from array|object to array|stdClass
description:
PHPC-1709 introduced a tentative {{array|object}} return type hint for [Serializable::bsonSerialize()|https://www.php.net/manual/en/mongodb-bson-serializable.bsonserialize.php]. In practice, PHPC only allows the method to return an array or [stdClass|https://www.php.net/stdClass] instance (see: [phongo_bson_encode.c|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/1.15.3/src/phongo_bson_encode.c#L114]).

If possible, we should consider narrowing this return type while it's still tentative.

This also came up in [mongodb/mongo-php-library#1077|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-library/pull/1077#discussion_r1205056840] for PHPLIB-1122.

summary: Retry KMS requests on transient errors
Running a bulk write using pipeline updates and let options causes segmentation fault
Use zend_result return type in valid iterator function for PHP 8.4+
description:
[php/php-src@9726721|https://github.com/php/php-src/commit/9726721560a3abe611ac534f00f9609ca67102ea] changed the return type of the {{valid}} function in {{zend_object_iterator_funcs}}. 

This was observed as a build warning in a [failing Windows build|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/actions/runs/17774414825/job/50518391909?pr=1868#step:3:441] for PHPC-2611.

summary: Investigate changes in CDRIVER-4153: Begin Header Hygiene & Verification
description: Tracks https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/pull/2053. Make changes to work towards header hygiene goals described in CDRIVER-6085.

summary: Gossiping the cluster time from monitoring connections can result in loss of availability
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2798}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Enforce BSON nesting limit in field path tracking
description:
libbson has historically enforced a maximum nesting level of 100 via {{STACK_MAX}} in {{bson-json.c}}, which dates back to [mongodb/libbson@5a6e835|https://github.com/mongodb/libbson/commit/5a6e8355ae598086ccbf7020e61459d604b3bb84#diff-a4347c22c79bc10e58d7ae24078983338b58a473077743b31eaf8d4fc4f5bcf7R18-R24] (if not earlier). This is consistent with the [server limit|https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/reference/limits/#mongodb-limit-Nested-Depth-for-BSON-Documents].

PHPC enforces no such limit on its own, but doing so would be trivial if added to existing methods for field path tracking.

summary: Throw LogicException from Binary::getVectorType() and toArray()
description:
The initial implementation of {{Binary::getVectorType()}} and {{toArray()}} threw an InvalidArgumentException if either method was invoked on a Binary instance without the vector subtype. LogicException would be more appropriate, since this has nothing to do with the arguments (or lack thereof) passed to these methods. BadMethodCallException might also be appropriate, but PHPC doesn't extend that base class.

This shouldn't constitute a BC break, as the docs will only ever refer to LogicException (see: [php/doc-en#5370|https://github.com/php/doc-en/pull/5370]).

summary: Test in evergreen with PHP 8.5
description: PHP 8.5 has been added by PHPC-2663

summary: Out-of-source build: Don't necessarily generate .h files in the source tree
description:
The existing {{AC_CONFIG_FILES}} directive specifies absolute paths, which will configure several `.h.in` files into the same directory containing the templates, which can clutter the mongoc source tree.

Should: Generate the files in the build tree, if that happens to be a different directory from the source tree.

summary: Remove obsolete platforms from build toolchain
description:
This platforms are no longer used to build the extension.

Remove 
 * rhel8-power8
 * 
rhel8-zseries

 

https://github.com/10gen/mongo-php-toolchain/blob/master/.evergreen/config.yml#L187-L199

summary: Remove legacy PHP versions from build toolchain
description:
This PHP versions are no longer supported by the extension and the library

Remove
 * PHP 7.4
 * PHP 8.0

[https://github.com/10gen/mongo-php-toolchain/blob/master/build-linux-toolchain.sh#L21-L23]

summary: Utilize zend_array_is_list() in php_phongo_is_array_or_document()
description:
[{{php_phongo_is_array_or_document()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/1.15.2/src/phongo_bson_encode.c#L55] iterates through array keys to differentiate between list and associative arrays.

PHP 8.1 introduced a {{zend_array_is_list()}} function ([php/php-src#6070|https://github.com/php/php-src/pull/6070]), which has several performance improvements. We should consider using that function (backporting if necessary) or incorporating some its optimizations.

summary: Avoid string length calculations for bson_append calls
description:
Various {{bson_append}} calls throughout the driver specify {{-1}} as the key length, which directs libbson to call {{strlen()}} on the function argument. For situations where we know the string length (e.g. string literal), we should explicitly specify the string length.

The {{BSON_APPEND}} macros discussed in the [libbson documentation|http://mongoc.org/libbson/current/creating.html] might also be reasonable if we can rely on the compiler to optimize {{strlen()}} calls for string literals.

summary: Avoid dynamic property assignment in phongo_add_exception_prop()
description:
While implementing BulkWriteCommandExcepiton in PHPC-2494, I observed the following deprecation notice at runtime:

bq. Deprecated: Creation of dynamic property Exception::$bulkWriteCommandResult is deprecated

This is a logic error on my part, but it would be helpful if {{phongo_add_exception_prop()}} asserted that the exception class has the property defined before attempting assignment. If so, we should consider how best to surface that error, since throwing another exception at that point might only complicate things from the application's perspective.

summary: Add typed readonly properties for value objects and events
clone on BSON\Javascript with scope triggers C assertion failure in libbson
Remove php_phongo_write_concern_to_zval and allow WriteConcern object in BulkWrite debug output
description:
As noted in [this comment thread|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/1042/files#r335104627] for PHPC-1411, {{php_phongo_write_concern_to_zval}} is only used when generating debug info for a BulkWrite object.

I believe we can remove this function entirely by modifying {{php_phongo_bulkwrite_get_debug_info}} to delete {{php_phongo_write_concern_to_zval}} in favor of calling {{phongo_writeconcern_init}}. The only side effect that I noticed from doing so is that the debug output of BulkWrite may now include a WriteConcern object instead of a flat array. I don't recall the original motivation for creating {{php_phongo_write_concern_to_zval}}, but I don't see an issue with the debug output having objects within it as that's what one might expect for userland PHP classes.

Note that a BulkWrite will only have a WriteConcern after it has been executed, so we have very little test coverage for this function. Most {{var_dump()}} expectations for BulkWrite objects are pre-execution and expect a NULL write concern. AFAIK, only {{write-0002.php}} provides code coverage, and even then only for a single write concern.

summary: Remove php_phongo_server_to_zval and allow Server object in Manager debug output
description:
{{php_phongo_server_to_zval}} is used by both the Manager and Server debug handlers. In the same spirit as the other linked issues, it may make sense to just have Manager include Server objects in its debug output instead of calling Server's debug handler directly.

Note that these debug handlers are not like dual-purpose functions on our value objects, which support both {{var_dump}} and {{var_export}}, since we intentionally decided not to support {{var_export}} on classes where we cannot implement {{__set_state()}} (see: [this comment|https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/PHPC-1023?focusedCommentId=2477904&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels%3Acomment-tabpanel#comment-2477904]).

summary: Remove php_phongo_read_concern_to_zval and allow ReadConcern object in Query debug output
description: {{php_phongo_read_concern_to_zval}} was originally introduced in [92348ca|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/92348caffcdff84506d1a93ba2ccfc97f9c329b3] for PHPC-448 and shared between the Query and ReadConcern debug handlers. It is now only used by the Query handler, so I think we can consider removing it in favor of adding a ReadConcern object to the Query's debug info.

summary: BulkWrite interface renamed to BulkWrite
Add __debugInfo() handlers for info classes
description: The database, collection, and index info classes for PHPLIB-70 would benefit from having a custom debug handler for PHP 5.6.

summary: Collection::aggregate() should default to use a cursor if possible
Ensure query/filter args always serialize as BSON documents
description:
CRUD methods that take filter arrays should ensure that they are cast to objects to guarantee serialization to a BSON document. Server 3.0+ is more strict about types, and BSON arrays may no longer be allowed where objects are expected.

Note that this is inconsistent for some commands (e.g. {{count}} was relaxed in SERVER-15456), but we should be consistent throughout.

For methods where filters are truly optional, we should simply omit the value rather than casting an empty array.

summary: CRUD update methods should use multi option
description: Currently, these methods specify a {{limit}} option, which is only used for delete operations.

summary: Insert results should contain IDs regardless of generation
description: Currently, only generated document IDs are tracked on the insert result classes. Per the CRUD spec, these should always have document ID values.

summary: Special handling for findAndModify null return values before 3.0
description:
While implementing the ["FindOneAndUpdate when no documents match with upsert returning the document before modification"|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/d1b89436658e78352839410d792626771f124da7/source/crud/tests/write/findOneAndUpdate.yml#L102] test case for [PHPLIB|https://github.com/10gen-labs/mongo-php-library-prototype/pull/4], I came across an inconsistency among server versions. This also applies to the corresponding [FindOneAndReplace test|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/d1b89436658e78352839410d792626771f124da7/source/crud/tests/write/findOneAndReplace.yml#L97]

The {{findAndModify}} command returns an empty BSON document on 2.4 and 2.6, while {{null}} is returned on 3.0. I couldn't find a specific SERVER ticket to cross-reference here, but I know this was the result of some refactoring (possibly related to SERVER-17372). Solving this will likely require manual inspection of {{lastErrorObject.updatedExisting}}.

summary: Implement UpdateResult::getUpsertedCount()
Collection::bulkWrite() updateMany should require update operators
Library relies on BSON being a vendor prefix, while our vendor should be "MongoDB"
DropCollection should not throw exception for nonexistent collection
ListIndexes should not throw exception for nonexistent collection
Segfault during FindOne test
